projects.title: 2Takt Blandning
projects.tagline: Get the two-stroke fuel mix right — every time
description: Old two-stroke engines on boats, jet skis and mopeds need the right oil-to-petrol ratio — get it wrong and you either wear the engine or foul the plugs. 2Takt Blandning turns the sums into a couple of taps: pick your jerrycan, pick your percentage, read the exact amount of oil.
It's a mobile-first PWA styled after the Swedish archipelago, built to be used standing on the jetty next to the tank.
features: Exact oil amount for any tank size and mix ratio
Mobile-first and installable as a PWA
Swedish archipelago look — calm, readable, offline-friendly

projects.title: Alkoholkalkylator
projects.tagline: A theoretical BAC estimator — honest about what it cannot know
description: Alkoholkalkylator ("Alcohol Calculator") estimates theoretical blood alcohol content with the Widmark formula and charts how the value changes hour by hour, drink by drink.

It is deliberately framed as an educational tool, and says so loudly: every number is a theoretical estimate built on population averages, the app measures nothing, and it must never be the basis for decisions about driving, work or safety. That honesty shaped the design as much as the maths did.
features: Widmark-formula estimate charted over time
Clear safety framing — estimates only, never a go/no-go tool
Tested calculation engine, accessibility-minded UI
Runs entirely in the browser

projects.title: Baltic Catch
projects.tagline: A kid-friendly fishing game set in the Baltic Sea
description: Baltic Catch is a fishing game for kids set in the Baltic: drop your line, feel for the bite and reel in the local species. Designed to be playable everywhere — desktop, mobile or tablet — straight in the browser.
Like most of my games it is deliberately lightweight: plain HTML5 and JavaScript, no installs, no accounts, just fishing.
features: Catch real Baltic Sea species
Simple one-touch fishing mechanics for kids
Playable on desktop, mobile and tablet
Runs directly in the browser — nothing to install

projects.title: Bastu-tävlingen
projects.tagline: Who lasts longest in the sauna? A playful competition for friends
description: Bastu-tävlingen ("The Sauna Competition") lets friends compete — with a wink — over who stays in the sauna longest: live leaderboard, personal timers, QR-code invitations, statistics and awards for the regulars.

It is a full self-hosted solution: frontend, API and database start with a single docker compose command, on SQLite or PostgreSQL. The demo link shows the public landing pages — the app itself runs on your own machine. And it is firm about one thing: this is a game between friends, never an endurance contest. Leave the sauna at the first sign of discomfort.
features: Live leaderboard and personal timers
Invite friends with a QR code
Statistics and awards
Self-hosted with one docker compose up — SQLite or PostgreSQL

projects.title: Blizzard Run
projects.tagline: Endless arcade skiing with the soul of a 90s classic — outrun the Blizzard Beast
description: Blizzard Run is my love letter to the ski games of the 90s, rebuilt from scratch as a modern browser game. You race down an endlessly generated mountain, weaving between trees and rocks, landing tricks and collecting coins — all while a rubber-banded Blizzard Beast hunts you down the slope.

Everything is procedural: the mountain guarantees a survivable path while escalating set-pieces, and all graphics and sound are generated at runtime with Canvas and WebAudio — the whole game ships without a single sprite sheet or audio file.
It is also a full PWA: install it, play it offline, and work through five game modes, seven power-ups, cosmetic unlocks and fourteen achievements.
features: Endless procedural mountain with a guaranteed safe path
Chase AI: the Blizzard Beast rubber-bands to keep every run tense
5 game modes including seeded Daily Challenge and Time Trial
7 power-ups, cosmetics, trails, emotes and 14 achievements
Accessibility options: reduced motion, high contrast, left-handed layout
Installable offline PWA — zero dependencies, no build step

projects.title: Bokstavspussel
projects.tagline: A Swedish spelling puzzle for kids — drag the letters, build the word
description: Bokstavspussel ("Letter Puzzle") is a spelling game I built for young kids learning to read Swedish. Drag colorful letter tiles into slots to spell words, with three difficulty levels that grow with the child — from three-letter words up to real tongue-twisters.

The whole game is one self-contained HTML file: no server, no libraries, works offline on any tablet. Audio feedback is synthesized in the browser and every correct word is celebrated with encouraging Swedish phrases.
features: Drag-and-drop letter tiles designed for small fingers
Three difficulty levels, remembered between sessions
Synthesized sound effects and encouraging feedback
Single HTML file — works offline, nothing to install

projects.title: CatchMate
projects.tagline: Everything fishing — catch log, competitions, AI species ID and clubs
description: CatchMate is the fishing companion I want in the boat: log a catch with photo, species, weight, spot and method, and let the app build the statistics — best times of day, best baits, even how air pressure affects the bite.

Around the log grows a social layer: fishing competitions with live leaderboards, clubs with shared records, badges and XP. An AI identifies the species from a photo and estimates length and weight. Next.js frontend, .NET backend — in active development.
features: Catch log with photos, map spots and auto-saved weather
AI species identification with length and weight estimates
Competitions with live leaderboards, clubs, badges and XP
Statistics: best times, best baits and air-pressure insights

projects.title: Dela
projects.tagline: Split costs, not friendships — group expenses in Swedish
description: Dela ("Share") is an expense-splitting PWA in Swedish for restaurants, trips, car pools and households: log expenses with categories, payers, participants and currencies, and let the settlement algorithm find the minimum number of payments — with Swish links ready to tap.

It scans receipts with AI-suggested line items (mocked for now, built to swap in real OCR), locks the exchange rate per expense, and draws its statistics with hand-rolled SVG charts in a colour-blind-validated palette. Mobile-first, installable and prepared for Capacitor packaging.
features: Minimum-payment settlement with ready-to-tap Swish links
Receipt camera mode with AI-suggested line items
Multiple groups and five currencies with locked rate per expense
Hand-built SVG statistics — line, pie and bar charts

projects.title: Depth Charge
projects.tagline: Hold to set the depth, release to destroy — submarine-hunting arcade
description: Depth Charge puts you on an auto-sailing patrol boat with enemy submarines lurking below. The whole game lives in one mechanic: hold the button to set the explosion depth — hold longer, sink deeper — then release to drop the charge. Sink every submarine with as few bombs as possible.

It grew into a full arcade package: a campaign with a boss every fifth level, endless survival, time attack and a seeded daily challenge, plus coins, XP and boat upgrades from blast radius to sonar. The engine is hand-built Canvas 2D with camera shake, and every sound is procedural Web Audio — no audio files at all.
features: One-button depth mechanic — hold to aim deep, release to drop
Campaign with bosses, survival, time attack and a daily challenge
Boat upgrades: blast radius, bomb capacity, speed and sonar
Hand-built Canvas engine, procedural audio, installable PWA

projects.title: Disco Bowl: Neon Strike
projects.tagline: Bowling night meets disco fever — 1 to 4 players, lasers, mirror balls and strikes
description: Disco Bowl turns a bowling alley into a full-blown disco: neon lights, lasers, a spinning mirror ball and real-time synthesized disco music. Up to four players take turns on the same device, each with their own avatar, shoes and ball.

The equipment actually matters — grip, glide, weight and spin feed into a physics model with a power meter and sweet-spot timing. Random comedic events (someone slips, the ball ends up on the ceiling) keep it fun without ever being unfair.
Built with pure HTML5 Canvas and WebAudio, pseudo-3D perspective and zero dependencies. The scoring and physics modules are plain testable JavaScript.
features: 1–4 players hot-seat on one device
Power meter with sweet-spot timing, spin and aim control
Equipment stats that genuinely affect ball physics
Real-time synthesized disco soundtrack and light show
Classic bowling scoring with strikes, spares and the 10th frame
21 AI-generated art assets in a unified neon theme

projects.title: FårJagDra
projects.tagline: May I tow this? Checks your car + trailer against Swedish licence rules
description: FårJagDra ("May I Tow?") answers a question every trailer owner in Sweden has fumbled with: may this car pull this trailer on a plain B licence, or does it take B96 or BE? Enter two registration numbers and the rule engine checks the weights and gives a verdict with the reasoning spelled out.

The MVP is live and runs on mock vehicle data while the registry API integration is pending — the unit-tested rule engine sits behind an async service, built to swap in the real data source without touching the UI.
features: Checks a car + trailer combination against B, B96 and BE rules
Explains the verdict — which weight limit decides and why
Unit-tested rule engine, ready to swap mock data for a real registry API

projects.title: Flow Local
projects.tagline: Press a hotkey, speak, get clean text at your cursor — 100% local, no cloud
description: Flow Local is dictation for Windows that never leaves your machine: press a global hotkey, speak in English or Swedish, and the text appears at your cursor — transcribed by faster-whisper and cleaned up by a local LLM through Ollama.
No cloud APIs, no subscriptions, no audio ever leaving the computer. Built for people who want to write with their voice without shipping their words to a datacenter.
features: Global hotkey — dictate into any application
faster-whisper speech recognition for English and Swedish
Local LLM cleanup via Ollama: punctuation, casing, filler removal
Text pasted straight at the cursor
Completely offline — nothing leaves the machine

projects.title: Food Tracker
projects.tagline: Snap a photo of your plate — AI estimates the calories
description: Food Tracker started with a simple annoyance: logging meals by hand is tedious enough that nobody keeps doing it. So I built a tracker where you photograph your food and GPT-4o Vision breaks it down into ingredients with estimated calories, protein and fiber — all editable before you save.

Behind the React frontend sits an Express API with SQLite storage, a calendar view of every day's meals, daily weight logging and an automatic calorie-deficit calculator that turns your weigh-ins into a running energy balance.
The backend has a proper test harness (Vitest + Supertest) and runs fine without an API key — the AI endpoints simply switch off.
features: AI photo analysis: per-ingredient calories, protein and fiber
Editable breakdowns — the AI suggests, you decide
Calendar and day views with daily goal tracking
Weight log with automatic calorie-deficit calculation
REST API over SQLite with a full test suite
projects.estimatedCompletion: Core features complete — production hardening in progress

projects.title: FutureNest
projects.tagline: See what fund fees really cost your savings over time
description: FutureNest visualises the question every saver should ask: what does the yearly fund fee actually cost me? Set your monthly amount, horizon and fee, and it charts how the fee eats into compound growth over the years — and how much more you would keep with a cheaper alternative.
The whole app is a single HTML file with no dependencies: open it, drag the sliders, watch the curves move.
features: Interactive chart of savings growth with and without fees
Shows the total cost of a fee over the full horizon
Single HTML file — nothing to install, no accounts
All calculations run locally in the browser

projects.title: Grillmästaren
projects.tagline: A BBQ timer that knows core temperatures — and reminds you to let the meat rest
description: Grillmästaren ("The Grill Master") is the app I wanted at the grill: pick your cut, pick your doneness, and get the right core temperature with a timer — including the resting phase people always skip.
It is one single HTML file you can bookmark on your phone and use next to the grill, no connection required once loaded.
features: Core temperature guide per cut and doneness
Cooking timer plus a dedicated resting timer
Single HTML file — instant to load, works offline

projects.title: Garden Planner AI
projects.tagline: Plan your garden with AI and augmented-reality overlays
description: A concept for a web app that helps you plan what to grow, where and when. Point your camera at a garden bed and see AI-suggested planting layouts as augmented-reality overlays, tuned to your climate zone and the Swedish growing season.
Still on the drawing board — the goal is to combine plant databases, local weather data and image recognition into a planner that feels like a garden designer in your pocket.
features: AI-generated planting suggestions per climate zone
AR overlay preview of beds and layouts
Season calendar with sowing and harvest reminders

projects.title: Sveriges Jaktlexikon
projects.tagline: A Swedish hunting encyclopedia that answers: may I hunt today?
description: Sveriges Jaktlexikon ("Sweden's Hunting Encyclopedia") gathers the huntable Swedish species in one fast app: identification, tracks, droppings, antlers, reproduction, range and curiosities, each with its own image gallery.

The part I care most about is the season engine: pick your county and a date and it answers "may I hunt today?" — green cards for open seasons, red for protected, with licence-hunt species flagged. The season data is fact-checked against the Swedish hunting regulation at county level.
features: Species pages with galleries and expandable fact sections
"May I hunt today?" — answered per county and date
Month-by-month season calendar for every species
Search, favourites and no login — data stays in your browser

projects.title: Kajak Race
projects.tagline: Rhythm-paddling kayak racing in the Hudiksvall archipelago
description: Kajak Race went from drawing board to finish line: a fast, colourful arcade kayak race set in the Hudiksvall archipelago. The controls are the whole game — you paddle by alternating two keys in an even rhythm, paddle harder on one side to steer, and if you just mash the keys you catch a crab and wobble.

Race two players on one keyboard against AI rivals across three tracks with different weather, dodging seals, swans, logs, jet skis and the angry gulls that go after the leader. Like my other games it is a single HTML file — double-click and play.
features: Rhythm-based paddling: even strokes for speed, one-sided strokes to steer
Two players on one keyboard plus AI rivals in four difficulty levels
Three archipelago tracks with sun, dusk and fog
Six customisable kayaks, power-ups and touch paddling on mobile

projects.title: Kubik
projects.tagline: See how big a cubic metre really is
description: Kubik answers a deceptively simple question: how much is a cubic metre, really? Pick a material — water, firewood, gravel, sand, soil or concrete — and a volume from 0.1 to 100 m³, and it renders it to scale next to people, cars, garages and houses, with weight and real measurements.

It also untangles the Swedish firewood measures — solid, stacked and loose cubic metres — that confuse every first-time firewood buyer. Favourites, dark mode, shareable URLs and a print-friendly view included.
features: Scale-true visualisation of any volume from 0.1 to 100 m³
Six materials with weight per m³, uses and trivia
Explains the three Swedish firewood measures
Ready-made examples in wheelbarrows, trailer loads and truck loads

projects.title: Missile Command
projects.tagline: A pixel-faithful remake of the 1980 arcade classic — in your browser
description: My remake of Atari's Missile Command, built to a written spec with the goal of being pixel-faithful to the 1980 original: a 256×231 internal buffer, fixed 60 Hz timestep, the classic color rotation every two waves, and the iconic unwinnable ending.

Defend six cities with three missile bases against ICBMs, MIRVs, smart bombs, bombers and satellites. Works with mouse on desktop and tap on mobile, installs as an offline PWA, and keeps a local top-10 high score table — just like the arcade.
features: Faithful wave design: ICBMs, MIRVs, smart bombs, bombers, satellites
Authentic scoring with 1×–6× multiplier and bonus cities
256×231 internal resolution with era-correct color cycling
Mouse and touch controls, installable offline PWA
Local top-10 high score table

projects.title: MovieIndex
projects.tagline: Your local movie files, turned into a searchable library with posters and metadata
description: MovieIndex scans a folder full of video files and turns it into something that looks like a streaming service: posters, ratings, cast, directors — all fetched from TMDB and cached locally in three tiers so rescans are instant.

It groups multi-disc and multi-version movies under one card, parses titles and years out of messy filenames, and offers real-time search across title, year, director, cast and rating. Hit play and the movie opens in your system player.
A Python/Flask backend with a vanilla JS frontend, complete with a pytest suite for the scanner.
features: Recursive scan with smart filename → title/year parsing
TMDB metadata and posters with 3-tier caching
Groups multiple versions of the same movie
Real-time search including rating filters like ">7"
One-click playback in the OS default player

projects.title: OnlineKvitto
projects.tagline: Photograph paper receipts, let AI file them — never miss a warranty
description: OnlineKvitto ("Online Receipt") takes the paper receipts that fade in a drawer and puts them online: photograph the receipt, an AI extracts the store, items and totals, and everything becomes searchable — with warranty tracking and reminders on top.

It is designed as a self-hosted Next.js and PostgreSQL app: a job queue living in the database, bilingual full-text and fuzzy search tuned for OCR noise, and a storage layer ready to move from local disk to S3. Freshly designed, now being built.
features: Snap a receipt — AI extracts store, line items and totals
Full-text and fuzzy search built to survive OCR noise
Warranty tracking with reminders before they run out
Self-hosted: Next.js, PostgreSQL and Prisma behind Auth.js

projects.title: Bokstavsdrag
projects.tagline: Reading and spelling for ages 6–8 — teaching letter sounds, not letter names
description: Bokstavsdrag ("Letter Drag") is a Swedish reading and spelling game for kids aged 6–8, built around a pedagogical idea: children learn to read faster when they learn the sounds letters make rather than their names.

Kids drag letters to build words across three difficulty levels. Letter sounds are played through speech synthesis by default, but the game supports dropping in your own recorded phoneme MP3s for a more natural voice — a feature I documented so any parent can do it.
One self-contained HTML file, fully offline, designed tablet-first.
features: Teaches Swedish letter sounds (phonemes), not letter names
Three difficulty levels from short to long words
Custom recorded letter sounds or built-in speech synthesis
Fully offline single-file game — tablet-first design

projects.title: Östersjöns Fiskar
projects.tagline: A field guide to the Baltic Sea's most common fish
description: Östersjöns Fiskar ("Fishes of the Baltic") is a guide to the 14 most common species in the Baltic Sea — their lives, habitats, spawning periods and how to fish them responsibly — plus the invasive species currently spreading through the sea.
Built with Next.js and Tailwind and pre-rendered to a fully static site: no servers, instant pages, and adding a new species is just adding one JSON file.
features: 14 species with habitat, spawning and responsible-fishing guidance
Covers the invasive species on the rise in the Baltic
Statically pre-rendered — fast, serverless and free to host
New species = one JSON file

projects.title: Pappersflyget
projects.tagline: Glide a paper plane as far as you can — and watch out for the eagle
description: Pappersflyget ("The Paper Plane") is an endless glider: hold to rise, release to dive, and keep a fragile paper plane in the air while obstacles stream past and an eagle takes an unhealthy interest in you.

Three difficulty modes change everything from wind gusts to obstacle density — from the calm "Lugn bris" to the chaotic "Storm" where the eagle actively hunts. Best distance is saved locally, and the whole game is a single HTML file that runs anywhere.
features: One-touch glide physics — easy to learn, hard to master
Three difficulty modes with wind, gusts and a hunting eagle
Best-score persistence and a mobile-first fullscreen design
Single self-contained HTML file, no dependencies

projects.title: PictureSorter
projects.tagline: Bring order to years of photo folders — automatically
description: PictureSorter is a C# desktop utility born from a very real problem: years of camera dumps and phone backups scattered across folders. It sorts photo collections into a clean structure automatically, so the family archive is browsable again.
features: Automatic sorting of large photo collections
C# desktop application for Windows

projects.title: Räknekul!
projects.tagline: Counting, plus and minus for six-year-olds — with a Swedish voice that reads every task
description: Räknekul! ("Counting Fun!") is a math game I made for kids around six who are just learning to count. Three modes — count the objects, add two groups, subtract — with a big friendly number pad so no keyboard is needed.

A Swedish voice reads every task aloud, right answers get cheerful audio praise, and ten correct answers in a row earn a confetti celebration. Playable directly in the browser, installable to the home screen, and free of any external dependencies.
features: Three modes: counting, addition and subtraction (up to 10 or 20)
Swedish text-to-speech reads each task aloud
Large on-screen number pad — no keyboard needed
Confetti rewards and easy/hard levels
Works offline, installable on tablets

projects.title: ReList
projects.tagline: Create once, reuse forever — checklist templates for groceries, packing and everything recurring
description: ReList is built on one insight: most lists are the same list, over and over. So instead of rewriting your grocery list every week, you create a template once and spin off a fresh run each time — the original stays untouched, completed runs go to history.

It has grown into a full offline-first PWA: a shopping mode with giant text and wake lock for the store, a packing mode that sums weights, voice input, a barcode scanner backed by Open Food Facts, QR start links and self-contained template sharing.
There is even an optional AI assistant for generating templates — but everything works completely offline without any account or API key. Vanilla JavaScript, no build step, bilingual Swedish/English.
features: Reusable templates: groceries, packing, car service, routines
Shopping mode with giant text and screen wake lock
Packing mode with per-item weights and totals
Voice input, barcode scanner and QR start links
Offline-first PWA with JSON export/import
Light/dark themes, six accent colors, Swedish + English

projects.title: Robebox
projects.tagline: First-generation self-hosted music player — superseded by Robebox 2
description: Robebox was my first take on replacing streaming subscriptions with my own MP3 collection: an ASP.NET Core API streaming a local library to a React PWA with a Spotify-style interface — artists, albums, search, queue, playlists, favorites and history.

It enriches artists with MusicBrainz metadata and cover art, streams over the LAN or remotely via Tailscale, and taught me everything about audio streaming, range requests and metadata pipelines that went into its successor.
Robebox is now archived: the ideas live on in Robebox 2, a complete TypeScript rewrite built for a 17,000-track collection.
features: Range-request MP3 streaming from your own library
Artist/album browsing, search, queue and playlists
MusicBrainz enrichment with permanent caching
Remote listening via Tailscale

projects.title: Robebox 2
projects.tagline: A self-hosted Spotify replacement for a 17,000-track collection — rebuilt from the ground up
description: Robebox 2 is the deliberate rewrite of my music server, designed around one real collection: roughly 17,000 files on a NAS. A TypeScript monorepo with a Fastify API, React 19 SPA and SQLite via Drizzle — scanned, verified and serving the whole library with a production build around 118 kB gzipped.

It goes far beyond version 1: household profiles with PINs, Daily Mixes, radio mode and a Time Capsule powered by a fully offline recommendation engine, plus Robebox Connect — control playback on any device from any other device over Socket.IO, Spotify-Connect style.

There is an in-app tag editor with undo, automatic WMA transcoding through ffmpeg, a four-step cover art chain, and a NAS-safe scanner with prune protection so a disconnected drive never wipes the library.
features: Full library on NAS: 17,000 tracks, 3,800+ artists, 151 imported playlists
Household profiles with PIN login
Daily Mixes, radio and Time Capsule — recommendations computed fully offline
Robebox Connect: cross-device playback control in real time
In-app tag editor with undo and WMA transcode cache
PWA with Media Session — works like a native player on mobile

projects.title: RobeFit
projects.tagline: Calorie and nutrition tracking on the Swedish national food database, with AI photo logging
description: RobeFit is my second-generation nutrition tracker, this time built on Livsmedelsverket's official Swedish food database for accurate local nutrition data instead of AI estimates alone. Photo logging with AI is still there — but now it matches against real database entries.
An ASP.NET Core 10 backend with a React 19 frontend, goal management and progress tracking. It carries forward everything I learned from Food Tracker.
features: Livsmedelsverket food database for accurate Swedish nutrition data
AI photo logging matched against real database entries
Goal setting and progress tracking
ASP.NET Core 10 + React 19 stack

projects.title: RoberMan
projects.tagline: 90s disco Bomberman — drop the bass and the bombs
description: RoberMan is Bomberman reimagined as a 90s eurodance fever dream: classic grid-based bomb tactics wrapped in disco colors with Web Audio-generated eurodance music pumping underneath.
The entire game lives in a single HTML file and is playable directly on GitHub Pages. Trap your opponents, kick bombs, grab power-ups — and try not to blow yourself up before the drop.
features: Classic grid-based Bomberman tactics
Eurodance soundtrack generated with the Web Audio API
Power-ups, chain explosions and escalating rounds
Single HTML file, playable online instantly

projects.title: RobHouseBuilder
projects.tagline: Sketch a house frame, get an exact lumber list — with an AI design assistant on the side
description: RobHouseBuilder came out of planning a real build: I wanted to sketch a simple house frame and immediately know how much lumber to buy. You draw the plan view on a snap grid — walls, doors, windows — set the roof pitch with a slider, and watch the gable section update live.

The lumber math is deliberately deterministic: rafters, ridge, studs and battens are computed with plain formulas you can verify, and exported as CSV that opens correctly in Swedish Excel. The AI layer is kept on a leash — GPT-4o-mini can suggest design changes and gpt-image-1 can render your cottage, but the server validates and clamps everything so the numbers stay honest.
features: Plan-view canvas with snap grid, inner walls, doors and windows
Live gable/truss section driven by width and roof pitch
Deterministic lumber formulas — the AI never touches the math
Grouped material list with CSV export for Swedish Excel
AI design suggestions and image rendering of the finished house

projects.title: Sänka Skepp
projects.tagline: The classic Battleship, rebuilt modern — with an AI that actually thinks
description: Sänka Skepp ("Sink the Ships") is the classic Battleship as a modern PWA: drag-and-drop fleet placement, explosions, lingering fires, splashes and a shaking board when a ship goes down.

The hard AI plays with a probability map over every possible ship placement, remembers its hits and hunts efficiently — or hand the device over and play two on one screen with a pass-and-play handover. Every sound, including the ambient music, is synthesised with Web Audio; there is not one audio file in the game.
features: Three AI levels, topped by a probability-map hunter
Two players on one device with a hand-over screen
Drag-and-drop placement with touch support and rotation
Stats, eight achievements, synthesised sound — installable and offline

projects.title: SjöCalc
projects.tagline: The boater's toolbox — marine converters, calculators and weather
description: SjöCalc ("SeaCalc") gathers the boating life's numbers in one PWA: eleven marine unit converters — knots, nautical miles, fathoms, Beaufort and more — six boat calculators for fuel burn, range, anchor rode, load, battery time and fill-up cost, and a smart search where typing "25 knop" gives every relevant conversion at once.

Around the maths sits real seamanship: an Open-Meteo forecast distilled into a boating-weather index, a harbour guide with sea marks, signal flags and right-of-way rules, a clickable boat that teaches the terminology and a three-level sea quiz. Works fully offline.
features: Smart search: type "25 knop" or "5 famnar", get answers instantly
Eleven converters and six boat calculators with quick tables
Boating-weather index built on Open-Meteo forecasts
Harbour guide, sea quiz and knowledge bank — offline-ready PWA

projects.title: SkafferiManager
projects.tagline: FIFO pantry management with barcode shopping mode and AI recipe ideas
description: SkafferiManager keeps track of what is actually in the pantry: every item follows FIFO with expiry indicators, purchases sync intelligently against current stock, and an in-store shopping mode lets you scan barcodes and swap items as you shop.

Underneath is a three-project ASP.NET Core 9 solution with EF Core and SQLite, a GS1/EAN article catalog seeded with a real product dataset, interchangeable-article relationships that fold into stock math, and a REST API with Swagger. GPT-4o-mini chips in with article suggestions and recipe ideas based on what is about to expire.
features: FIFO inventory with expiry tracking and item splitting
Smart purchase lists that sync against actual stock
Barcode-driven in-store shopping mode
GS1/EAN article catalog with interchangeable articles
AI-suggested recipes from soon-to-expire ingredients
REST API with Swagger and a full audit history

projects.title: Slumpa
projects.tagline: Every random picker you need — dice, wheels, cards and draws in one PWA
description: Slumpa ("Randomize") collects the everyday randomness tools in one Swedish PWA: dice from D4 to D100, drawing straws, raffle tickets, a wheel of fortune, a full 52-card deck, name pickers, number ranges and option choosers — for games, classrooms, clubs and settling arguments.

Everything stays on the device: saved lists and history live in localStorage, the randomness comes from crypto.getRandomValues with rejection sampling, and every sound effect is synthesised with WebAudio — there is not a single audio file in the bundle.
features: Eight tools: dice, straws, raffle, wheel, cards, names, numbers, options
History with one-tap re-roll and saved reusable lists
Installable PWA with offline support, dark mode and Swedish/English
Fair randomness via crypto.getRandomValues — no data leaves the device

projects.title: TankKoll
projects.tagline: Log your fill-ups, learn what your car really burns
description: TankKoll ("Tank Check") logs your fill-ups and works out what your car actually consumes — rolling average over recent tanks, cost per distance, monthly graphs — and compares the real number against the manufacturer's WLTP figure, so you can see how far off the brochure is.

A new fill-up takes under fifteen seconds, with distance and consumption previewed live while you type. Everything stays in your browser: no accounts, no server, no tracking — just a free, installable PWA.
features: Real consumption from odometer and litres — not guesswork
WLTP comparison against the factory figure
Statistics in three tabs with monthly graphs and cost breakdowns
Local-only data, installable as a PWA, completely free

projects.title: Tum till Millimeter
projects.tagline: "Grab me a two-by-four" — Swedish lumber sizes decoded to millimetres
description: Tum till Millimeter ("Inches to Millimetres") decodes Swedish carpenter-speak: type 2x4, "tvåfyra", 45x95 or "stud for an interior wall" and get the planed and rough-sawn dimensions in millimetres, with an illustration, typical uses and related sizes.

Around the search sits a live inch-to-millimetre converter that understands decimals and fractions like 3/4 and 1 1/2, a full dimension table, a fourteen-term builder's glossary and an FAQ — plus favourites, history, dark mode and offline support as an installable PWA.
features: Interprets carpenter language — 2x4, tvåfyra, trekvarts, 45x95
Live inch ⇄ mm converter with decimals and fractions
Lumber tables with planed/rough-sawn toggle and illustrations
Builder's glossary and FAQ — installable, works offline

projects.title: Gräddagar
projects.tagline: Was this month's heating bill high because of the weather? Degree-days know.
description: A small tool that answers a very Swedish question: was the heating bill high because the house is wasteful, or just because it was cold? It compares a location's degree-days against the climate normal and translates the difference into estimated savings or extra cost.
Warmer-than-normal and colder-than-normal periods get clear color-coded indicators, so you can separate weather effects from real changes in consumption. One clean, mobile-first HTML file.
features: Degree-day comparison against climate normals
Savings and extra-cost estimates in kronor
Color-coded warmer/colder indicators
Clean mobile-first design in a single file

projects.title: Vattenskoteräventyret
projects.tagline: Ride a jet ski from Hudiksvall harbor into the archipelago — dodge boats, collect stars
description: Vattenskoteräventyret ("The Jet Ski Adventure") is a cheerful endless runner set on home waters: you ride a jet ski out of Hudiksvall harbor and into the archipelago, switching lanes and jumping over buoys, waves and rocks while boats and cliffs demand quick reactions.

It is deliberately kid-friendly — a friendly sea monster swims along, the speed ramps up gently, three hearts forgive mistakes and every thousand points earns confetti. One HTML file, touch controls, works on any tablet.
features: Lane-switching and jumping with low and high obstacles
Gentle difficulty ramp with three hearts
A friendly sea monster companion
Local high score and confetti celebrations
Single-file game with tablet touch controls

projects.title: VindrosAnalys
projects.tagline: A premium stock-analysis dashboard on live Börsdata — shorts, insiders, buybacks and more
description: VindrosAnalys is my stock-analysis cockpit: a Blazor Server dashboard synced against the Börsdata API, presenting Nordic equities with advanced filtering, historical date navigation and per-stock deep dives — prices, estimates, P/E bands, reports, insider trades and calendars.

It handles the API politely with a sliding-window rate limiter and background price sync, tracks short positions and buybacks across the market, and can optionally run AI analysis on individual stocks. The whole thing wears a navy-and-gold dark theme and ships with Docker deployment and CI.
Recent work has focused on a proper mobile experience with two-step list/detail navigation.
features: Live Börsdata sync with rate limiting and background updates
Filterable dashboard with historical date navigation
Per-stock details: prices, estimates, reports, insider trades
Short positions and buyback tracking across the market
Optional AI-generated stock analysis
Docker deployment with CI pipeline
projects.estimatedCompletion: In active use — mobile polish ongoing

projects.title: Weight Converter
projects.tagline: A small, focused unit converter for weights
description: A compact Python tool for converting between weight units — one of those utilities that does a single thing and gets out of the way. Kept public as a simple, readable example of a small finished project.
features: Converts between common weight units
Minimal, readable Python
